title: Fluid conduit with improved circumferential expansibility
abstract: Conduits having an anisotropic capacity for stretching are described herein. Such conduits can be deployed in vascular systems to provide a pathway of care, as they can be expanded circumferentially to provide a cross-section for blood flow that is appropriate for the size of the patient. In embodiments, the conduits described herein expand spontaneously along the pathway of care when exposed to normal blood pressure. For example, the conduit can spontaneously expand when inserted in the left or right cardiovascular structures, veins, and/or arteries.
claims: Claims 1. A method for coupling a first plenum and a second plenum with an expansible conduit, the method comprising: isolating a section of anisotropic material having a primary stretch direction; recircularizing the section of anisotropic material to form a conduit, wherein the primary stretch direction is arranged along a circumference of the conduit; and coupling the conduit to the first plenum and the second plenum to form a fluidic coupling therebetween. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the anisotropic material comprises PTFE. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the anisotropic material comprises an extruded tube. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the anisotropic material comprises an extruded sheet. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the first plenum is a pulmonary artery and the second plenum is a right ventricle. 6. The method of claim 1 further comprising circumferentially dilating the conduit. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein dilating the conduit comprises exposing the conduit to a pressure of about 15-30 mm Hg. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein dilating the conduit comprises increasing the diameter of the conduit along a size pathway corresponding to the growth rate of a patient. 9. A conduit made by the process of: isolating a section of anisotropic material having a primary stretch direction; recircularizing the section of anisotropic material to form a cylinder, wherein the primary stretch direction is arranged along a circumference of the cylinder; and attaching the ends of the recircularized portions of the cylinder to one another at a seam along a longitudinal length of the conduit. 10. The conduit of claim 9, wherein the anisotropic material comprises PTFE. 11. The conduit of claim 9, wherein the anisotropic material comprises an extruded tube. 12. The conduit of claim 9, wherein the anisotropic material comprises an extruded sheet. 13. The conduit of claim 9, wherein the cylinder has a radius of about 10 mm. 14. The conduit of claim 9, wherein the anisotropic material is configured to plastically deform when exposed to a pressure of about 15-30 mm Hg. 15. The conduit of claim 14, wherein the plastic deformation occurs along a size pathway corresponding to the growth rate of a patient.
